confirmations.reason: Project-Level AGENTS.md is exactly a repo-scoped instruction file that gives the agent standing instructions and project-specific context on top of global defaults — a direct match.
Plugins explicitly bundle skills, app integrations, and MCP servers into reusable workflows for Codex, which maps directly to 'bundle reusable capabilities/instructions the agent can load.'
Custom Agents via TOML files let users define different model configurations and instructions, directly shaping the agent's persona/system-prompt/output style.
Per-Agent Model Selection explicitly allows assigning specific models (auto or pinned) to each agent — a direct realization of model selection capability.
MCP (Model Context Protocol) is described as the standard way to connect external tools and context providers to Codex — a direct match to the external tool/connector protocol capability.
MCP Server CLI Management directly provides add/manage operations for MCP servers via CLI, covering install, configure, and discovery — a direct match.
OAuth MCP Login directly authenticates to MCP servers via OAuth using the codex mcp login flow — an exact match to MCP authentication capability.

Container Caching caches configured containers to speed up task startup, not repeated prompt/context payloads. This is infrastructure/execution caching, not prompt or context caching to cut token cost/latency.
Subagent Workflows describes Codex spawning specialized agents in parallel to handle parts of a task and consolidating results — a direct realization of subagent/delegated-agent capability.
Multi-Agent Workflows via Codex CLI as MCP server + Agents SDK describes coordinating multiple agents that share work — a direct match to multi-agent teams and messaging capability.

Automations describes recurring background tasks that run unattended and report findings — this is closer to scheduled/background execution than to named, repeatable, multi-step workflow sequences the user defines and saves.
Git Worktree Isolation explicitly runs each automation in an isolated worktree to prevent interference — directly realizing parallel isolated workspaces.
Cloud Environments provides managed cloud/container environments controlling what Codex installs and runs, with caching and secrets — a direct match.
Automations explicitly runs tasks unattended in the background and reports findings, directly realizing background/async execution.
Standalone Automations are independent scheduled tasks that run on their own without a conversation thread — a direct match to scheduled/triggered runs.
The Sandbox feature is described as a constrained environment letting Codex run local commands autonomously without unrestricted access — directly realizing sandboxed execution.
Approval Policy controls when Codex must stop and ask before crossing sandbox boundaries — directly realizing tool permission controls (approve/deny what the agent is allowed to do).
Network Access Control explicitly enables/disables outbound network access with an optional allowed-domain list — a direct and specific match.

DNS Rebinding Protection guards against a specific network attack vector within the sandbox, not against malicious instructions injected via tool/web content into the prompt. These are adjacent security concerns but not the same capability.
Codex Security is described as a security-review plugin and cloud service that scans repositories for vulnerabilities and validates findings — a direct match to security scanning capability.

A Custom Setup Script runs user-defined scripts for complex environment configuration, but it does not represent integration with a defined dev-container standard (e.g., devcontainer.json). It is a generic script hook, not a dev-container integration.
Built-in Run Tracing emits structured records of model calls, tool calls, handoffs, guardrails, and custom spans — directly realizing audit logging/telemetry for agent actions.
AI Code Review describes Codex reviewing GitHub PR diffs and posting a standard GitHub code review — a direct realization of automated code review capability.
Evals API allows configuring and running evaluations of model outputs programmatically, including against external benchmarks — a direct match to evals/testing harness capability.
Multi-Device Session Handoff allows resuming an active Codex session from any signed-in device — directly realizing resume/fork session (the resume aspect at minimum).
Remote Connections lets you use Codex from another device/machine to control an agent remotely, including starting/continuing runs — a direct match to remote control capability.
Task Completion Notifications directly provides alerts when Codex finishes a task or needs input — an exact match to the notifications capability.

Codex for Linear is a connector that lets you delegate work from Linear issues by mentioning @Codex — directly realizing chat/issue-tool surface integration (Linear is an issue/project tool analogous to the described capability).

GitHub Integration is a specific integration for PR review and async cloud tasks, not a definition of the access surfaces (terminal, IDE, web, cloud, mobile) where the agent can be used. It is one connector, not a surface taxonomy.
